---
title: "2026-07 Lab Meeting"
author: "Jacob Krol"
format:
  revealjs:
    css: theme.scss
---

## Gene fusions

<br>

![](./images/gene-fusion-cartoon.png){fig-align="center"}

## Tumor-driving fusions

::: {.center}
**Recurrent** fusions can drive tumor progression
:::

![](./images/erg-tmprss2.png){fig-align="center" width=5%}



## Differential recurrency

![](./images/fusion-differential-recurrency.png){fig-align="center" width=70%}

## Multi-sample calling gap

<br> 

![](./images/single-v-multi-sample-calling.png){fig-align="center" width=70%}



## Tumor heterogeneity

::: {.center}
Low fusion signal
:::

<br>


:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="10%"}
:::
::: {.column width="40%"}
::: {.center}
![**Single-sample**](./images/blue-person-cropped.png){fig-align="center" width=50%}
:::
:::
::: {.column width="40%"}
![](./images/fractional-tumor-cells.png){fig-align="center" width=30%}
:::
::: {.column width="10%"}
:::
::::


## Multiple samples increase sensitivity

::: {.center}
High aggregate fusion signal
:::

<br>

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="25%"}
::: {.center}
![**Single-sample**](./images/blue-person-cropped.png){fig-align="center" width=65%}
:::
:::
::: {.column width="25%"}
![](./images/fractional-tumor-cells.png){fig-align="center" width=30%}
:::
::: {.column width="25%"}
::: {.center}
![**Multi-sample**](./images/blue-person-cohort.png){fig-align="center" width=80%}
:::
:::
::: {.column width="25%"}
![](./images/fractional-tumor-cells-grid.png){fig-align="center"}
:::
::::

## Multiple samples increase specificity

::: {.center}
Ignore fusions with high normal signal
:::

<br>

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="25%"}
::: {.center}
![**Single-sample**](./images/black-person-cropped.png){fig-align="center" width=65%}
:::
:::
::: {.column width="25%"}
![](./images/fractional-tumor-cells.png){fig-align="center" width=30%}
:::
::: {.column width="25%"}
::: {.center}
![**Multi-sample**](./images/black-person-cohort.png){fig-align="center" width=80%}
:::
:::
::: {.column width="25%"}
![](./images/fractional-tumor-cells-grid.png){fig-align="center"}
:::
::::

<!-- ## Leveraging corroborating evidence

![](./images/corroborating-dna-and-rna-evidence.png){fig-align="center" width=40%} -->

## Single-sample depth dependence

![](images/k562-star-fusion-v-depth.png){fig-align="center" width=70%}




## Population fusion dataset

::: {.center}
4,647 total human genome samples
:::

![](./images/tissue-type-databases.png){fig-align="center" width=70%}

## Sample heterogeneity

![](../results/2026_04-pcawg_sample_counts/sample_counts_tissue.png){fig-align="center" width=70%}


## Fusion prioritization

<!-- - **Polymerization** score ranks fusions given multimodal data -->

<br>

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="33%"}

![](./images/fusion-search-space.png){fig-align="center" width=80%}
:::
::: {.column width="66%"}
![](./images/score-cartoon.png){fig-align="center" width=100%}
:::
::::

:::{.center}
:::{.small}
$$
\text{score} \approx \text{#reads}_{\text{tumor}} + \text{#samples}_{\text{tumor}} - \text{#reads}_{\text{normal}} - \text{#samples}_{\text{normal}}
$$
:::
:::

## Score prioritization evaluation

<!-- ::: {.small}
- Recurrent tumor fusion score is higher than random gene pairs
- PCAWG prostate fusion call (not recurrent) scores are right-skewed
::: -->

<br> 

:::{.center}
Prostate
:::

<!-- ![](../results/2025_11-score/prostate/null_v_pcawg_hist.log.no_pcawg.png){fig-align="center"} -->

![](./images/prostate-score-distribution.png){fig-align="center" width=70%}





## Recurrent normal vs. tumor classification


:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="60%"}
![](./images/score-threshold.png)
:::
::: {.column width="40%"}
![](../results/2026_06-roc_tumor_v_normal_tissue_specific/roc.no_rna.unreported_dropped.png){fig-align="center"}
:::
::::

## Inspecting tumor and normal scores

:::{.small}
:::{.center}
Tumor confidence order: ChimerKB > ChimerPub > ChimerSeq
:::
:::

![](./images/density-score-tumor-v-normal.png){fig-align="center" width=50%}


## Tissue dependence

:::{.center}
:::{.small}
Prostate
:::
:::

:::{.aside}
:::{.center}
Directly comparing scores across tissues is not a good idea; p-value is more informative
:::
:::

![](./images/prostate-score-distribution.png){fig-align="center" width=50%}

:::{.small}
:::{.center}
Blood
:::
:::

![](../results/2026_06-g2f-all_gene_pairs/chunks_scored/blood_scored.tsv.sorted.no_selfie.hist.png){fig-align="center" width=50%}



## Recurrent tissue classification

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="40%"}
<br>
![](./images/tissue-specific-labels.png){fig-align="center"}
:::
::: {.column width="60%"}
:::{.center}
![**PCAWG recurrent fusions**](./images/pcawg-recurrent-fusion-heatmap-legened-erg-tmprss2-box.png){fig-align="center" width=80%}
:::
:::
::::

## Tissue specificity score evaluation


:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}
<br>
![](./images/score-threshold-tissue-specific-labels.png){fig-align="center"}
:::
::: {.column width="40%"}
<br>
![](../results/2026_04-roc_tumor_and_normal_recurrent_fusions/roc_data-neg_tissue_agnostic/roc-best.png){fig-align="center"}
:::
::::

## Fusion discovery attemps


<!-- ![](../results/2025_11-score/kidney/null_v_pcawg_hist.log.no_pcawg.png){fig-align="center" width=40%} -->

![](..//results/2026_04-kidney_shpk_itgae_viz/plot.png){fig-align="center" width=50%}

<!-- :::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](../results/2025_11-score/kidney/null_v_pcawg_hist.log.png){width=80% fig-align="center"}
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](..//results/2026_04-kidney_shpk_itgae_viz/plot.png){fig-align="center"}
:::
:::: -->

:::{.center}
:::{.small}
Is SHPK-ITGAE a recurrent fusion?
:::
:::

## SHPK-ITGAE recurrency


<br>

:::{.center}
Recurrent, but not tumor driving
:::

<br>

| # Tumor samples | # Normal Samples | 
| --- | --- |
| 20 samplots | 11 samplots | 

## Confounders of discovery

<br>

:::{.center}
:::{.small}
High coverage regions
:::
:::

![](./images/samplot-high-coverage-artifact.png){fig-align="center" width=70%}

## Confounders of discovery

::: {.center}
:::{.small}
Highly expressed genes
:::
:::

![](./images/ndufa4l2-hpa-expression.png){fig-align="center" width=30%}

![](./images/samplot-ndufa4l2-nab2.png){fig-align="center" width=40%}


## Confounders of discovery

<br>

:::{.center}
:::{.small}
Inversion noise
:::
:::

![](./images/samplot-inversion-artifact.png){fig-align="center" width=70%}

## Exemplary samplot fusions

<br>

:::{.small}
:::{.center}
Lung cancer (LC2AD) fusion
:::
:::

![](./images/lc2ad-CCDC6--RET.png){fig-align="center" width=40%}

## Discovery challenges

1. High coverage confounders
2. Normal fusions are insufficiently penalized by score


![](./images/samplot-high-coverage-artifact.png){fig-align="center" width=70%}

## Discovery refinement process

:::{.small}
:::{.center}
- Prioritize fusions with high partner fraction (STAR-Fusion inspired)

$$
\text{partner_fraction}(x,y) = \frac{\text{#fusion reads}_{xy}}{\text{#fusion reads}_x} > 0.2
$$

- Guided alignment verification (FusionInspector; input is FASTQ)
:::
:::

![](./images/fusion-inspector.png){fig-align="center" width=70%}









## 1000G RNA problems

<br>

:::{.small}
:::{.center}
STAR-Fusion calls on 1000G RNA sample
:::

| #FusionName | JunctionReadCount | SpanningFragCount |
| --- | --- | --- |
| TIMM23--PARGP1 | 95 | 16 |
| TIMM23--RP11-574K11.29 | 34 | 7 |
| ... | ... | ... |
:::

<br>

:::{.small}
:::{.center}
Excord finds 0 fusion reads from BAM of same sample.
:::
:::

## Next steps


<br>

:::{.center}
:::{.small}
How to narrow search space by orders of magnitude?
:::
:::

![](../results/2026_06-g2f-all_gene_pairs/chunks_scored/blood_scored.tsv.sorted.no_selfie.hist.png){fig-align="center" width=70%}


## Next steps

:::{.center}
:::{.small}
What can be learned from well-studied fusions?
:::

![](./images/TMPRSS2--ERG.png){fig-align="center" width=40%}

![](./images/lc2ad-CCDC6--RET.png){fig-align="center" width=40%}
<!-- ![](./images/ALK--PTPN3.png){fig-align="center" width=40%} -->

:::

## Breakpoint awareness

![](./images/et2-breakpoint-scatter-by-strand.png){fig-align="center" width=70%}

## Breakpoint awareness

![](./images/et2-breakpoint-binned.png){fig-align="center" width=70%}




<!-- ## Discovery potential

![](./images/discovery-potential.png){fig-align="center" width=80%} -->

## Filtering with control cohort

::: {.center}
- 59,117 benchmark cancer cell line fusions
:::


:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](./images/haas-publication.png){fig-align="center" width=100%}
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](./images/haas-onekg-depth-no-title.png){fig-align="center" width=100%}
:::
::::



<!-- :::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="33%"}
![](../results/2025_09-haas_onekg/haas_onekg_readcount_histogram.png){fig-align="center" width=80%}
:::
::: {.column width="33%"}
:::
::: {.column width="34%"}
![](../results/2025_09-haas_onekg/haas_onekg_readcount_histogram_filt.png){fig-align="center" width=80%}
:::
:::: -->


## Appendix


## Data

::: {.small}
- Tumor and control (WGS & RNA-seq): 2,143 Pan-cancer Analysis of Whole Genomes (PCAWG) samples
- Control (WGS): 2,504 samples from 1000G project
- Total 4,647 human genome samples
:::

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](../results/2026_04-pcawg_sample_counts/sample_counts_tissue.png){fig-align="center" width=80%}
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](./images/1000-genomes.jpg){fig-align="center" width=70%}
:::
::::

:::aside
Variability in tissue, sequencing technology, and disease state across samples
:::

## Read-level resolution

- Read-level fusion evidence is stored for each sample

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="33%"}
![](./images/database-solid.png){fig-align="center" width=55%}
![](./images/stix-cropped.png){fig-align="center" width=55%}
:::
::: {.column width="66%"}

<br>

![](./images/fundamental-units-of-genefusion-evidence.png){fig-align="center" width=100%}
:::
::::

## Fundamental units of fusion evidence

![](./images/fundamental-units-of-genefusion-evidence-w-cohort.png)

$$
\text{score} = {\color{skyblue}{\text{#reads}}} + {\color{skyblue}{\text{#samples}}} - \text{#reads} - \text{#samples}
$$


## Normalization



::: {.small}
- Normalization accounts for population size and sequencing parameters (coverage)
:::

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](./images/sample-normalization.png){fig-align="center" width=60%}
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](../results/2026_01-toy-score-plots/tumor_read_score.png){fig-align="center" width=80%}
:::
::::


:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="40%"}
::: {.very-small}
$$
\text{score}_{\text{sample}} \in [0,1] = \frac{\text{#samples}}{\text{Population size}} 
$$
:::
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
::: {.very-small}
$$
\text{score}_{\text{reads}} \in [0,1] =
\begin{cases}
  \frac{\mathbb{E}[\text{max reads]} - |\text{#reads} - \mathbb{E}[\text{max reads}]|}{\mathbb{E}[\text{max reads}]} & \text{if #reads} \leq 2\cdot \mathbb{E}[\text{max reads}] \\[6pt]
  0 & \text{else}
\end{cases} 
$$
:::
:::

::: {.column width="30%"}

:::
::::

## Blood score distribution

<br>

![](../results/2026_06-g2f-all_gene_pairs/chunks_scored/blood_scored.tsv.sorted.no_selfie.hist.png){fig-align="center" width=70%}

## Bone score distribution

<br>

![](../results/2026_06-g2f-all_gene_pairs/chunks_scored/bone_scored.tsv.sorted.no_selfie.hist.png){fig-align="center" width=70%}

## Breast score distribution

<br>

![](../results/2026_06-g2f-all_gene_pairs/chunks_scored/breast_scored.tsv.sorted.no_selfie.hist.png){fig-align="center" width=70%}

## Esophagus score distribution

<br>

![](../results/2026_06-g2f-all_gene_pairs/chunks_scored/esophagus_scored.tsv.sorted.no_selfie.hist.png){fig-align="center" width=70%}

## Gallbladder score distribution

<br>

![](../results/2026_06-g2f-all_gene_pairs/chunks_scored/gallbladder_scored.tsv.sorted.no_selfie.hist.png){fig-align="center" width=70%}

## Headneck score distribution

<br>

![](../results/2026_06-g2f-all_gene_pairs/chunks_scored/headneck_scored.tsv.sorted.no_selfie.hist.png){fig-align="center" width=70%}

## Kidney score distribution

<br>

![](../results/2026_06-g2f-all_gene_pairs/chunks_scored/kidney_scored.tsv.sorted.no_selfie.hist.png){fig-align="center" width=70%}

## Liver score distribution

<br>

![](../results/2026_06-g2f-all_gene_pairs/chunks_scored/liver_scored.tsv.sorted.no_selfie.hist.png){fig-align="center" width=70%}

## Ovary score distribution

<br>

![](../results/2026_06-g2f-all_gene_pairs/chunks_scored/ovary_scored.tsv.sorted.no_selfie.hist.png){fig-align="center" width=70%}

## Pancreas score distribution

<br>

![](../results/2026_06-g2f-all_gene_pairs/chunks_scored/pancreas_scored.tsv.sorted.no_selfie.hist.png){fig-align="center" width=70%}

## Prostate score distribution

<br>

![](../results/2026_06-g2f-all_gene_pairs/chunks_scored/prostate_scored.tsv.sorted.no_selfie.hist.png){fig-align="center" width=70%}

## Sample heterogeneity

![](../results/2026_04-pcawg_sample_counts/sample_counts_tissue.png){fig-align="center" width=70%}





<!-- ## Single-sample caller low depth

![](images/k562-fusion-calls-v-depth-colored.png){fig-align="center" width=70%} -->

<!-- ## Tissue dependence

::::{.columns}
::: {.column width="80%"}
![](../results/2026_06-g2f-all_gene_pairs/chunks_scored/liver_scored.tsv.sorted.no_selfie.hist.png){fig-align="center" width=70%}
![](../results/2026_06-g2f-all_gene_pairs/chunks_scored/ovary_scored.tsv.sorted.no_selfie.hist.png){fig-align="center" width=70%}
![](../results/2026_06-g2f-all_gene_pairs/chunks_scored/prostate_scored.tsv.sorted.no_selfie.hist.png){fig-align="center" width=70%}
:::
::: {.column width="20%"}

:::{.center}
<br>
Liver

<br>
<br>

Ovary

<br>
<br>

Prostate
:::

:::
:::: -->
